calendar.matters:
Notice Of Application For Verified Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Aug-25-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ROSE MISHAAN OF CA TRAFFIC DEFENDERS, TEL. NO. 1-415-431-1313 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. TERRY SENNE, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 1-510-622-2118, APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER ELDON HODGIN'S Application For Verified Petition For Writ Of Mandate is DENIED. To comply with California's implied consent law, "a driver should clearly and unambiguously manifest the consent required by the law. Consent which is not clear and unambiguous may be deemed a refusal." (Garcia v. Department of Motor Vehicles (2010) 185 Cal.App.4th 73, 82, quoting Carrey v. Department of Motor Vehicles (1986) 183 Cal.App.3d 1265, 1270.) Here, the record demonstrates that Petitioner repeatedly refused to consent to treatment before making the ambiguous statement that "Your [sic] going to take my blood anyway so what's the point." The administrative hearing officer was entitled to conclude that Hodgin refused to submit to a chemical test, and correctly imposed the one-year suspension. Order and Judgment signed in open court. Judge: Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/sb
